items.title: Rondje KNVB-beker: veteranen maken verschil bij PSV en NEC
items.summary: Nog slechts vier wedstrijden telt het toernooi om de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. In Rondje KNVB-beker staan we donderdag stil bij de kwartfinales, met speciale aandacht voor PSV en NEC. Clubwatchers Tim Reedijk en Sander Janssen schuiven aan bij presentator Kalum van Oudheusden.

items.title: Marco van Ginkel zet op 33-jarige leeftijd punt achter carrière
items.summary: Marco van Ginkel heeft op 33-jarige leeftijd een punt achter zijn profloopbaan gezet. De voormalig speler van onder meer PSV en Vitesse zat sinds afgelopen zomer al zonder club.

items.title: Oosting hoopt op jawoord van Janssen
items.summary: In de buitenlandse topcompetities is Deadline Day voorbij, maar in enkele landen kunnen nog deals gesloten worden. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Slot: New signing Jacquet 'a very big talent'
items.summary: Arne Slot has described defender Jérémy Jacquet as a "very big talent" ahead of his summer move to Liverpool.

items.title: Man City to appeal rule barring Guéhi from final
items.summary: Manchester City are set to ask the English Football League for special permission to play Marc Guéhi in the Carabao Cup final, according to Pep Guardiola.

items.title: City fly past Newcastle into cup final with Arsenal
items.summary: Manchester City struck three times in the first half as they eased into the Carabao Cup final by completing a comfortable 5-1 aggregate win over Newcastle United.

items.title: 'Galatasaray huurt voor 30 miljoen verkochte rechtsback terug van Bayern'
items.summary: Galatasaray haalt Sacha Boey terug. De 25-jarige rechtsback komt op huurbasis over van Bayern München.

items.title: Why Jérémy Jacquet may be the ‘heir to Virgil van Dijk’ for Liverpool
items.summary: The 20-year-old is a ‘complete’ player who combines Marcel Desailly’s defensive ability with Laurent Blanc’s creativity By Get French Football News When you miss 18 months of football, there is a natural eagerness to make up for lost time. Jérémy Jacquet has certainly done that. This time last year Rennes recalled him from a loan spell at Ligue 2 side Clermont Foot and now he has been signed by Liverpool for £60m. The conditions were always ripe for Jacquet to succeed at Rennes, a club known for developing talent. But even by their standards, the 2005 generation is something special. Désiré Doué, Mathys Tel, Jeanuël Belocian, Lesley Ugochukwu and, come the summer, Jacquet will have all left Rennes, but not before pushing each other to greater heights during their formative years. Continue reading...

items.title: WK-deelname Ter Stegen serieus in gevaar door hamstringblessure
items.summary: De rentree van Marc-André ter Stegen bij Girona laat voorlopig op zich wachten. Er is een operatie nodig om zijn hamstringblessure te verhelpen. Daardoor is WK-deelname serieus in gevaar.

items.title: EFL will knock back Guardiola’s push for Guéhi to play in Carabao Cup final
items.summary: Cup rules state new signings must arrive before semi ‘Hopefully they can change it,’ City manager said Any attempt by Manchester City to clear Marc Guéhi to play in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal will be rebuffed by the English Football League. Guardiola questioned the rule which means Guéhi cannot feature in March’s final and said City would ask the EFL to reconsider. But there will be no change to a regulation introduced before this season. Continue reading...

items.title: Scotland leave out Kinghorn, Graham, Van der Merwe for Italy opener
items.summary: British and Irish Lion Blair Kinghorn has been dropped for Scotland's Six Nations opener against Italy on Saturday with Tom Jordan taking his place.

items.title: Celtic in advanced talks over Oxlade-Chamberlain
items.summary: Celtic are in advanced talks with former Arsenal and Liverpool midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain.

items.title: Hull KR's Peters 'interested' in taking England job for World Cup
items.summary: Hull KR coach Willie Peters has stated his interest in taking over as England boss for the World Cup.

items.title: Same again please Bruno - FPL team of the week
items.summary: Bruno Fernandes retains the captain's armband in the FPL team of the week after his 20-point haul in gameweek 24.

items.title: Newcastle’s Saudi vision is shrouded in bleak suspicion and unfulfilled promises | Jonathan Liew
items.summary: Vivid dreamscape sold to fans in 2021 is yet to materialise amid layers upon layers of bureaucracy, economics and geopolitics Layer two: Nick Woltemade, signed for £69m in the hot madness of summer, has stopped scoring. Anthony Elanga, a £55m winger, has struggled for game time and goals. Malick Thiaw, a £35m centre-half bought from Milan, keeps making basic errors. Last summer’s transfer window, conducted without a sporting director and with an outgoing chief executive, looks increasingly like a disaster. The football seems a little slower and less urgent these days, St James’ Park a little quieter and more anxious. Eddie Howe is basically holding this thing together with hugs and smiles. Layer three: turns out Alexander Isak lighted the exit path so that others might follow. Sandro Tonali’s agent decided to make a little mischief on transfer deadline day, putting Arsenal on alert. Perhaps Tonali will be the next painful transfer saga, perhaps Bruno Guimarães or Lewis Hall or Tino Livramento. The sporting director, Ross Wilson, is still getting his feet under the table. The chief executive, David Hopkinson, reckons Newcastle can be the best team in the world by 2030. They sit 11th in the Premier League. No signings arrived in January. Continue reading...

items.title: Man City's Carabao Cup final rule-change request to be rejected
items.summary: The EFL will not change its rules to allow Marc Guehi to play for Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final, Sky Sports News understands, despite appeals from Pep Guardiola.

items.title: Premier League darts schedule: Littler faces Van Veen on opening night
items.summary: The latest fixtures, results and schedule from the 2026 Premier League Darts season, where world No 1 Luke Littler and defending champion Luke Humphries are among those in action.

items.title: 'Relegation a blessing' but issues remain - Cardiff's reset
items.summary: Cardiff City's reset in League One has some fans saying relegation has been a "blessing" - but one ex-captain warns the same problems remain.

items.title: More questions than answers in challenging Newcastle season
items.summary: Holders Newcastle United were knocked out of the Carabao Cup by Manchester City on Wednesday night and a challenging season shows no sign of relenting.

items.title: Grab your popcorn -- it's Man City vs. Arsenal for the Carabao Cup, and maybe more
items.summary: Man City's win in the Carabao Cup semis sets the stage for a final vs. Arsenal, and a potentially thrilling end to the season.
